qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Privilege escalation via share boundary bypass in public PATCH operations
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a privilege escalation via publicPatchHandler replacing d.user with the share creator's user object (public.go:358), granting operations outside the share boundary. The advisory describes a different root cause: the shareinfo API endpoint (/public/api/shareinfo) returning download URLs without verifying the share password. Different endpoint, different missing check, different vulnerability mechanism.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal via symlink resolution allows unauthorized file system access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes symlink resolution without scope validation in resolvePathContext(). The advisory describes password protection bypass on the shareinfo endpoint. Completely different components, code paths, and root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a password protection bypass on the /public/api/shareinfo endpoint, which returns download URLs without verifying the share password (CWE-288/CWE-200/CWE-602). The privilege_escalation_share finding addresses a different issue (publicPatchHandler user context replacement at public.go:358). The path_traversal_symlinks finding addresses symlink resolution scope validation.
evidence: Advisory root cause: /public/api/shareinfo endpoint returns downloadURL without password verification
privilege_escalation_share root cause: d.user = shareCreatedByUser at public.go:358 (PATCH handler privilege escalation, not shareinfo password bypass)
path_traversal_symlinks root cause: filepath.EvalSymlinks() without scope validation in indexingFiles.go (unrelated component)
